cards.text: When defining a class template member function outside the class, you must provide the {{c1::template parameter declaration}} before the function definition.
When defining a member function outside a class template, use the syntax: template<typename T> {{c1::ReturnType ClassName<T>::}}functionName()
Class templates and their member functions are typically defined entirely in {{c1::header files}} because the compiler needs to see the full definition to {{c2::instantiate}} them.
Member functions of class templates are only {{c1::instantiated}} when they are {{c2::actually used}} in the program.

cards.front: Why are class template definitions typically kept in header files?
cards.back: Because the compiler needs to see the full definition to instantiate the template with specific types. If separated into .cpp files, the linker won't be able to find the instantiated versions.

cards.front: What happens if you define a member function in a class template but never call it?
cards.back: The member function is never instantiated by the compiler, so it won't generate any code for it.

cards.question: Complete the member function definition outside the class template.
cards.explanation: Need return type T and class name with template parameter Array<T>::, then function name.

cards.question: What's wrong with this code?
cards.answer: Missing the template parameter declaration before the function definition.

cards.question: Is this code valid?
cards.answer: Yes, this is valid. badFunc() is never instantiated since it's not called.
cards.explanation: Template member functions are only instantiated when used, so the error in badFunc() is never encountered.

Class templates require special organization:
  • Typically keep everything in header files (.h or .hpp)
  • Alternative: Use .inl (inline) files included at end of header
  • Cannot separate into .cpp files like regular classes
  • Reason: Compiler needs full definition to instantiate templates

cards.back: The process where the compiler generates concrete code from a template by replacing template parameters with actual types
